summary: TSM is fair to slightly attractive at $346 — elite business quality and improving tariff backdrop support a premium multiple, but the price already embeds ~26% revenue growth and leaves limited margin of safety against overseas fab and N2 dilution risk.
thesisStatement: TSMC is the bottleneck owner of leading-edge compute and advanced packaging, enabling it to compound earnings faster and longer than a typical foundry, even as it absorbs a massive 2026-2027 capex cycle.
thesisBullets: Dominant advanced foundry with ~70% global market share, widening competitive moat versus Samsung (7.2%) and SMIC (5.3%)
AI accelerator revenue already high-teens percent of 2025 revenue — structural, not optional upside
Management guides ~30% USD revenue growth for 2026, supported by Jan-Feb revenue tracking at +29.9% YoY
Net cash position of ~US$65.6B provides balance sheet resilience during heavy capex phase
US-Taiwan tariff reduction (20% to 15%) incrementally improves cost structure for US-bound shipments
variantPerception: The market correctly prices TSMC's quality but may underestimate how infrastructure-like the earnings base has become while overestimating near-term multiple expansion potential. The key blind spot is not quality (everyone sees it) but the tendency to dismiss capital intensity and overseas dilution as 'already known.' These factors are known but may not be fully reflected in the multiple the market will sustain if margins disappoint even modestly.

valuationLens.crossCheck: Trailing FCF yield (cautious — distorted by US$52-56B 2026 capex)
valuationLens.rationale: Forward P/E fits best for a profitable, net-cash, structurally differentiated business. EV/EBITDA flatters heavy reinvestment. Spot FCF yield is directionally useful but too distorted by the elevated capex program to serve as the main anchor.

currentValuationSnapshot.balanceSheetNote: Net cash ~US$65.6B. Cash and marketable securities NT$3.07T versus bonds payable ~NT$1.03T. Strong balance sheet supports heavy capex phase.

newsToModel.event: US-Taiwan tariff cut from 20% to 15% on qualifying tech trade
newsToModel.impact: Modestly positive for non-AI demand and customer cost structure on US-bound shipments. Slight upward adjustment to base-case net margin (~+0.3pp) and slight reduction in bear-case probability.
newsToModel.affectedScenario: Base and Bear — supports base margin, reduces bear probability

newsToModel.event: TSMC confirmed ~70% global foundry market share in 2025 (up from 64.4% in 2024)
newsToModel.impact: Reinforces structural competitive moat. Samsung fell to 7.2%. Supports premium multiple versus peers but largely priced in.
newsToModel.affectedScenario: All — multiple floor support, no direct EPS change

newsToModel.event: New Tainan fab planned for 2028 completion
newsToModel.impact: Extends capital intensity story through 2028-2029. FCF yield will remain suppressed longer than some investors may expect. Creates ~1,400 direct jobs.
newsToModel.affectedScenario: Bull — supports long-term revenue capacity; all cases — FCF suppression continues

newsToModel.event: Management warned overseas fab ramp dilutes gross margin 2-3% initially, 3-4% later; N2 ramp dilutes 2-3%
newsToModel.impact: Caps aggressive margin assumptions. Keeps bear case alive even if revenue growth remains strong.
Real-time confirmation that ~30% 2026 growth framework is tracking. Supports base case over bear case.

newsToModel.event: Board approved US$44.96B capital appropriation and US$30B TSMC Global injection
newsToModel.impact: Confirms management conviction in multi-year demand but also confirms rising capital intensity and FCF suppression.
newsToModel.affectedScenario: All — FCF-based bull argument weakened in near term

scenarios.label: Bear
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue ~US$150B (+22% YoY); net margin ~42.8%. AI healthy but consumer weakness, tariff friction, or worse-than-expected overseas/N2 dilution prevents margin leverage from matching revenue growth.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: ADR EPS ~US$12.38 at 24x forward P/E. Market de-rates from premium to fair-for-cyclical territory.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: Consumer/auto demand stays soft despite tariff relief, overseas fab dilution exceeds guidance bands, or non-AI revenue disappoints enough to question the breadth of the growth story.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue ~US$159B (+30% YoY); net margin ~44.3%. Management's growth framework broadly holds, advanced-node and CoWoS demand stays tight, margin dilution is manageable.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: ADR EPS ~US$13.58 at 28x forward P/E. Premium-but-disciplined multiple reflecting quality and capital intensity.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: AI demand remains robust, monthly revenue continues tracking near 30% growth, margin dilution stays within guidance bands, and tariff environment remains stable or improves further.

scenarios.label: Bull
scenarios.operatingAssumption: 2026 revenue ~US$166B (+35% YoY); net margin ~45.8%. AI demand supply-constrained in TSMC's favor, pricing discipline holds, margin dilution milder than guided.
scenarios.valuationAssumption: ADR EPS ~US$14.65 at 31x forward P/E. Market re-rates toward infrastructure premium on clean execution.
scenarios.whatMustBeTrue: AI demand stays supply-constrained, advanced packaging remains a bottleneck in TSMC's favor, margin dilution proves milder than guidance, and improving tariff environment supports broader demand recovery.

currentPriceImplies: At US$345.98, the stock implies approximately US$13.1 of forward ADR EPS at a 26-27x multiple, mapping to roughly US$154-155B of 2026 revenue at a 44% net margin (~26% growth versus 2025). This is a strong outcome — above the bear case and solidly in base-case territory, though not pricing in the full base case. The market is giving TSMC credit for sustained AI demand and above-industry growth. What it is not fully paying for is a clean bull path with contained dilution. The tariff reduction has slightly improved the implied picture, but the core setup remains: reasonable if base case holds, not deeply undervalued.
currentPriceImpliesBrief: Price embeds ~26% growth at 44% margins — solid but not pricing in full base case upside.
provisionalConclusion: TSM looks fair to slightly attractive, not cheap. Business quality is elite and the 2026 demand backdrop is strong enough to justify a premium valuation. The tariff cut is incrementally positive but does not fundamentally change the setup. The stock offers ~10% upside to base fair value and ~31% upside to bull fair value against ~14% downside to bear fair value. At $346, risk/reward is acceptable but not compelling for aggressive new positioning. The hidden support is TSMC's infrastructure-like earnings base and improving tariff environment; the key risk is treating those positives as permission to ignore capital intensity and dilution.
technicalCommentary: RSI(14) has recovered to approximately 61.7, up from mid-40s a week ago. Price bounced from support near $328-336 and rallied 2.3% to $345.98 on above-average volume. However, price remains below the 20-day SMA (~$358.61). The 50-day SMA remains above the 200-day SMA (golden cross intact). The stock has bounced off support but has not yet reclaimed the 20-day SMA or confirmed a full momentum turn. Better than the prior neutral reading, but not yet a clean favorable entry signal.
risks: Overseas fab ramp diluting gross margin by 2-4% over 2026-2027, potentially worse if yields disappoint
N2 node ramp diluting 2026 gross margin by an additional 2-3%
Non-AI demand (smartphone, auto, IoT) remaining soft despite tariff relief
Rising capital intensity suppressing FCF yield and potentially capping the valuation multiple
Geopolitical risk around Taiwan Strait remains a structural overhang
US tariff environment could reverse or not extend further, limiting the tailwind
Customer concentration in top AI/HPC accounts creates order-timing volatility
catalysts: Q1 2026 earnings on April 16 — a high-end print would confirm above-consensus start to the year
Monthly revenue tracking near or above 30% growth through Q2 would strengthen the base case
Overseas fab margin dilution proving milder than guided would be a significant positive surprise
Further US-Taiwan tariff reductions would support non-AI demand and multiple expansion
Advanced packaging (CoWoS) remaining supply-constrained in TSMC's favor supports pricing power
N2 node ramp executing on schedule with better-than-expected yields
monitorNext: Q1 2026 earnings (April 16): revenue and gross margin versus US$34.6-35.8B / 63-65% guidance
March 2026 monthly revenue (~April 10): whether Jan-Feb ~30% YoY growth pace sustains
Updated overseas fab and N2 margin dilution guidance in Q1 earnings call
Advanced packaging capacity utilization: whether CoWoS/SoIC stay supply-constrained
Tariff environment evolution: whether 20%→15% cut extends further or stabilizes
Non-AI demand trajectory: smartphone, auto, IoT stabilization or continued softness

sourcesUsed.label: Taipei Times — TSMC nets ~70% of 2025 foundry market
Taipei Times — TSMC new Tainan fab for 2028
Simply Wall St — US-Taiwan tariff cut analysis

history: 2026-03-19: Updated analysis at US$345.98. Fair to slightly attractive. Thesis intact. Tariff cut from 20% to 15% is modestly positive. Bear $297 / Base $380 / Bull $454. Technical neutral-improving.
2026-03-16: Initial DeepValue analysis at US$338.31. Fair to slightly attractive. Thesis intact. Bear $294 / Base $375 / Bull $446. Technical neutral.
